geographicSummary: District Two stretches from the Clintonville and Beechwold neighborhoods south of Route 161 up through the northeast edge of the city toward the Westerville and New Albany borders. It has the highest median household income among the nine districts and borders several affluent suburban communities.

pointsOfInterest.name: Columbus Park of Roses (Whetstone Park)
pointsOfInterest.description: 13-acre public rose garden with over 350 varieties, heritage garden, herb garden, and perennial plantings.

pointsOfInterest.name: Studio 35 Cinema & Drafthouse
pointsOfInterest.description: Independent art house theater with two screens, full bar, and in-house pizza kitchen in Clintonville.

pointsOfInterest.name: Clintonville Farmers Market
pointsOfInterest.description: Seasonal weekly farmers market featuring local produce, baked goods, and community gathering space.

pointsOfInterest.name: Olentangy River Trail
pointsOfInterest.description: Multi-use trail for hiking and biking along the Olentangy River corridor through the district.

pointsOfInterest.name: Whetstone Library
pointsOfInterest.description: Columbus Metropolitan Library branch in Clintonville, a frequent venue for area commission meetings.

pointsOfInterest.name: Blendon Woods Metro Park
pointsOfInterest.description: 653-acre park on the district's eastern edge with forests, wetlands, nature center, and Walden Waterfowl Refuge.

areaCommissions.name: Clintonville Area Commission
areaCommissions.meetingSchedule: First Thursday of each month, 7:00 PM

snapshots.source: U.S. Census Bureau ACS 5-Year via Census API (backfill_2021.py)

data.data_note: Median income, rent, and home value are population-weighted averages of block-group medians — approximations, not true district medians. See /data/ for methodology.
